title: Count Objects in a Set
description: Count items in a pictured set to determine the total number.
condition: Given a finite set of visible objects and asked "How many?"
response: State the total number of objects using one-to-one counting.
label: Production / Skill
example: Count 5 pattern blocks in the puzzle and state "5."
notes: Includes understanding that the last number said represents the total.

title: Evaluate True/False Statement
description: Determine whether a mathematical statement about a picture is true or false.
condition: Given a declarative statement about quantities, shapes, or positions in a picture.
response: Select "yes" if true and "no" if false based on the picture.
label: Principle / Model
example: If there are 5 blocks shown, the statement "There are 6 blocks" is false → select "no."
notes: Requires integrating counting or positional reasoning with logical evaluation.

title: Identify Longer Object
description: Determine which of two objects has greater length.
condition: Given two objects with different lengths and asked to find which is longer.
response: Select or state the object with greater length.
label: Production / Skill
example: Between two rectangles of lengths 8 cm and 5 cm, select the 8 cm rectangle as longer.
notes: Requires visual or physical comparison of length.

title: Identify Rectangle by Attributes
description: Recognize a rectangle based on having 4 sides and 4 corners (right angles), regardless of orientation or proportions.
condition: Given a 4‑sided shape and asked whether it is a rectangle.
response: Classify the shape as a rectangle if it has 4 sides and 4 corners.
label: Concept
example: A long skinny shape with 4 sides and 4 right angles is identified as a rectangle.

title: Identify Shorter Object
description: Determine which of two objects has lesser length.
condition: Given two objects with different lengths and asked to find which is shorter.
response: Select or state the object with smaller length.
label: Production / Skill
example: Between two rectangles of lengths 8 cm and 5 cm, select the 5 cm rectangle as shorter.

title: Identify Triangle by Attributes
description: Recognize a triangle based on having 3 sides and 3 corners.
condition: Given a 3‑sided shape and asked whether it is a triangle.
response: Classify the shape as a triangle if it has 3 sides and 3 corners.
label: Concept
example: A shape with 3 straight sides is identified as a triangle.
notes: Students may infer missing sides in real-world objects.

title: Interpret Positional Words Above/Below
description: Determine relative vertical position using the terms "above" and "below."
condition: Given two objects in vertical arrangement and a statement using "above" or "below."
response: Judge whether the positional statement correctly describes their relative positions.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If a triangle is higher than a square, the statement "The triangle is above the square" is true.
notes: Common error is reversing meanings of above and below.

title: Recognize Shapes in Real-World Objects
description: Identify familiar geometric shapes embedded in real-world objects or pictures.
condition: Given a complex picture containing everyday objects.
response: Select or mark objects that match a specified geometric shape (e.g., rectangle or triangle).
label: Production / Skill
example: Circle the rectangular book cover; cross out the triangular ruler.
notes: Students generalize shape attributes to environmental objects.
